observations.summary: Dagon's voice is described as the sound of bedrock grinding beneath mountain roots, paralleling the motif of deep, primal, and immovable forces in the narrative.

source_evidence.quote: "If ever you should alter your tone," Dagon said, his voice the sound of bedrock grinding in the dark below the roots of mountains, "merely knock three times, ask twice, then wait. I shall come. I am the cadence you seek to outrun."

observations.target_hint: Dagon's voice and presence echo the archetype of primordial, foundational forces represented by bedrock and mountains in the narrative, symbolizing an unyielding, ancient power Dan must confront.
